text: **Anna:** Hello!
**Anna:** I have {great|素晴らしい/すごい|/ɡreɪt/|Great job today!} news.
**Anna:** My best friend from my {hometown|地元の町|/ˈhoʊmtaʊn/|I miss my hometown.} is coming here to Washington DC!
**Anna:** I can't wait to {catch up|再会する/近況を聞く|/kætʃ ʌp/|Let's catch up over coffee.} with her!
**Anna:** Oh, I {gotta|〜しなければならない（口語）|/ˈɡɑːtə/|I gotta go now.} go.
**Anna:** Her train {arrives|到着する|/əˈraɪvz/|The train arrives at 3 PM.} in ten minutes.
**Anna:** Penelope!
**Anna:** Holla
**Penelope:** I am really {happy|幸せな/うれしい|/ˈhæpi/|I am happy to see you.} to see you.
**Anna:** Me too.
**Anna:** How was your {trip|旅行/旅|/trɪp/|It was a nice trip.}?
**Penelope:** I was fine.
**Anna:** Let me help you with your {bags|バッグ/荷物|/bæɡz/|Can you carry these bags?}.
**Penelope:** I'm really {excited|興奮している/わくわくしている|/ɪkˈsaɪtɪd/|I am excited about the concert.} to be in Washington, DC!
**Penelope:** I can't wait to hear about {everything|すべて/全て|/ˈevriθɪŋ/|Tell me everything about your day.}.
**Anna:** Penelope, I have so much to tell you!
**Anna:** Let's go to my {apartment|アパート/マンション|/əˈpɑːrtmənt/|My apartment is small but cozy.}.
**Anna:** We can talk over a hot cup of tea.
**Penelope:** I love your apartment building, Anna.
**Penelope:** Is your {rent|家賃/賃料|/rent/|The rent is high in this area.} expensive?
**Anna:** Well, I have a {roommate|同居人/ルームメイト|/ˈruːmmeɪt/|My roommate is very friendly.}.
**Anna:** So we split the rent.
**Penelope:** Oh that's right
**Penelope:** Is your roommate nice?
**Anna:** Marcia is the {nicest|最も親切な|/ˈnaɪsɪst/|She is the nicest person I know.} person I know in this city.
**Anna:** Sometimes she {worries|心配する/悩む|/ˈwʌriz/|I worry about my exams.} too much.
**Anna:** And she says I'm the {messiest|最もちらかした/最も散らかりやすい|/ˈmesɪst/|My room is the messiest.} cook she knows.
**Anna:** But we are great roommates.
**Penelope:** So Anna, is it hard to make {friends|友達/仲間|/frendz/|I made new friends at school.} in DC?
**Anna:** At first, it was hard, but now Marsha is a good friend.
**Anna:** And there's {Pete|人名}.
**Anna:** Of all the people I know in DC, Pete is the most {serious|真面目な/深刻な}
**Anna:** And also the {silliest|馬鹿げた/おかしな}
**Penelope:** He sounds {interesting|興味深い/面白い}
**Anna:** Jonathan and Ashley are two other good {friends|友人/仲間|/ˈfrendz/|My friends help me a lot.}
**Anna:** In the city, they are the {friendliest|最も親切な} people I know.
**Anna:** They always help me when I need it.
**Penelope:** Your friends sound great
**Penelope:** So, tell me about your {job|仕事/職業|/dʒɑːb/|I love my job very much.}
**Anna:** I love my work.
**Anna:** I make a children's show called The {Time Traveling|タイムトラベル} Tree House.
**Penelope:** Anna, that is the best job for you.
**Penelope:** Do you remember when we were little?
**Penelope:** We played in that old {tree house|ツリーハウス} behind my family's house for hours
**Anna:** It is really good to talk to you.
**Anna:** New friends are good, but old friends are the best.
**Anna:** I know.
**Penelope:** Our {hometown|故郷} isn't the same now.
**Penelope:** You are not there.
**Anna:** No crying.
**Anna:** Penelope, why don't you move here and live with me and Marcia?
**Penelope:** Anna, I can't leave our hometown.
**Penelope:** You forget
**Penelope:** I love my job too.
**Penelope:** I didn't forget.
**Penelope:** You are the most famous {turkey|七面鳥/七面鳥肉|/ˈtɜːrki/|We eat turkey on Thanksgiving.}
**Penelope:** Thanks, Anna.
**Anna:** Come on, let's go eat dinner at one of DC's most famous {restaurants|レストラン/食堂|/ˈrestrɑːnts/|We love eating at nice restaurants.}
**Penelope:** Awesome!
**Anna:** I have a great {apartment|アパート/マンション|/əˈpɑːrtmənt/|My apartment is very cozy.}
**Anna:** I {love|愛する/好きである|/lʌv/|I love my family.} my work and I have {awesome|素晴らしい/素晴らしい|/ˈɔːsəm/|What an awesome day!} friends.
**Anna:** I am the {luckiest|最も幸運な|/ˈlʌkiəst/|Today is my luckiest day.} woman in Washington, DC
**Anna:** Until next time
